text.content: How to Generate Flawless AI Images (Google's New Model!)

The video by Jack Roberts introduces "Nano Banana 2," Google's new image generation model, highlighting its improvements in speed, control, intelligence, and cost-effectiveness. It focuses on achieving consistent and high-quality AI-generated images through "JSON prompting" within an "Anti-Gravity" (similar to OpenClaw) environment, leveraging Gemini 3.1 Pro to write these detailed JSON prompts from simpler requests. The process involves creating an organized project structure in Anti-Gravity, defining system prompts, and using custom skills to guide the image generation workflow. Optimizing costs using external platforms and continuous skill improvement through iterative feedback are also discussed.
• Nano Banana 2: Google's new image model offering faster, smarter, and cheaper image generation with improved text accuracy and Google Search integration.
• JSON Prompting: Crucial for achieving consistent, high-quality, and controlled AI image outputs, allowing for granular specification of image attributes.
• Anti-Gravity/Gemini for Prompt Generation: Using a smart agent like Gemini 3.1 Pro within Anti-Gravity to convert vague text descriptions into detailed JSON prompts.
• Skill-based Workflows: Custom skills define the image generation process, enabling reproducible results and continuous improvement through feedback loops.
• Cost Optimization: Utilizing external execution platforms for potentially lower costs and organizing output into structured folders.
